title: First Missing Integer
hints: Think about what the first missing positive integer could be. It must be between 1 and N+1 (where N is the array size).
Since we need O(n) time and constant space, we can't use extra arrays or sorting.
Use the array itself as a hash table. For each number, mark its presence by making the value at that index negative.
First, replace all non-positive numbers with a value outside the range [1, N] (like N+2).
Then, for each number in the array, use its absolute value as an index and mark that position as negative.
Finally, scan the array to find the first positive value - its index+1 is the first missing positive integer.
